method: Systematic concurrency-safety audit of all ADEQUATE FMs. Each FM's defense layers classified as SAFE (creation-time gate, read-only check, atomic operation, hash verification) or CORRELATED (depends on shared mutable state: git index, working tree, file locks). Reclassify CORRELATED FMs per L-1237.
expect: 5 ADEQUATE FMs downgraded to PARTIAL per L-1237; remaining 8 survive concurrency-correlation audit
actual: 5/13 ADEQUATE FMs reclassified ADEQUATE→PARTIAL (FM-01, FM-04, FM-09, FM-14, FM-19). Remaining 8 ADEQUATE FMs verified concurrency-SAFE. New FMEA distribution: 0 INADEQUATE, 17 MINIMAL, 12 PARTIAL, 6 ADEQUATE, 4 UNMITIGATED (39 total). Net: -5 ADEQUATE, +5 PARTIAL.
diff: Expected 5 downgrades + 8 survivors: CONFIRMED. No unexpected downgrades in remaining ADEQUATE FMs. Concurrency-safety taxonomy validated: creation-time gates (FM-22), hash verifications (FM-05, FM-11), read-only monitors (FM-02, FM-30), content-based blocks (FM-03), filesystem checks (FM-06), and serialized-through-index.lock checks (FM-18) are all resilient to N≥5 shared-state correlation.
verdict: CONFIRMED — L-1237 reclassification is complete and correct

reclassifications.name: Mass git staging
reclassifications.reason: S427 cross-session bypass — per-session guard doesn't block other sessions from staging
reclassifications.correlation_type: shared git index — staging is per-session but deletions affect shared working tree

reclassifications.name: Git index corruption
reclassifications.reason: S476/S478/S480 3x recurrence — index corruption caused by concurrent git operations racing for index.lock
reclassifications.correlation_type: shared git index.lock — concurrent reads/writes create corruption window

reclassifications.name: Concurrent deletion storm
reclassifications.reason: S427 detection worked but recovery is manual only — detect-without-recover is PARTIAL not ADEQUATE
reclassifications.correlation_type: detection OK, recovery gap — automated rollback missing

reclassifications.name: WSL loose object corruption
reclassifications.reason: S476/S478 continued post-ADEQUATE — filesystem-level failure outside git guard scope
reclassifications.correlation_type: external to git — defense layers cannot prevent WSL filesystem-level corruption

reclassifications.name: Logical overwrite
reclassifications.reason: S470/S472 false positives from stale_write_check.py — defense itself becomes friction source
reclassifications.correlation_type: defense-as-friction — false positives cause workarounds that bypass the defense

survivors.name: WSL filesystem corruption (CORE.md)
survivors.rationale: git fsck reads immutable .git/objects; stat guard reads file metadata. Both read-only, no shared mutable state dependency.

survivors.name: Ghost lesson resurrection
survivors.rationale: Ghost detection compares staged filenames against archive list (deterministic). Pre-commit block serialized through index.lock. compact.py auto-unstage is single-session.

survivors.name: Belief mutation (CORE.md)
survivors.rationale: Genesis hash comparison is content-addressable — same content = same hash regardless of concurrency. contract_check.py validates structural invariants.

survivors.name: PreCompact state loss
survivors.rationale: Checkpoint detection uses filesystem mtime (per-file, not shared state). Accumulation guard counts files (read-only). Each session reads its own checkpoint.

survivors.name: Genesis replay
survivors.rationale: Genesis hash is content-addressable. Written once per genesis bundle, verified by string comparison.

survivors.name: Concurrent lesson collision
survivors.rationale: lesson_collision_check.py runs inside pre-commit hook, which acquires index.lock. Serialization ensures HEAD is current when check runs. claim.py provides advisory pre-check.

survivors.name: Creation-maintenance asymmetry
survivors.rationale: open_lane.py gate runs at lane creation time, before any shared state interaction. L-1237 explicitly identifies creation-time gates as concurrency-safe.

survivors.name: Cross-layer cascade blind spot
survivors.rationale: cascade_monitor.py reads tool outputs and filter states. Pure monitoring — no mutable state dependency.

concurrency_safety_taxonomy.creation-time-gate: Defense prevents the action at creation time, before shared state is involved (e.g., open_lane.py staleness gate)
concurrency_safety_taxonomy.hash-verification: Defense compares content-addressable hashes — same content = same result regardless of concurrency
concurrency_safety_taxonomy.read-only: Defense only reads state, never modifies shared mutable state
concurrency_safety_taxonomy.content-based: Defense compares file content or names against known lists — deterministic
concurrency_safety_taxonomy.filesystem-check: Defense reads per-file metadata (mtime, size) — not shared across sessions
concurrency_safety_taxonomy.serialized-through-lock: Defense runs inside git index.lock serialization — only one session at a time
concurrency_safety_taxonomy.CORRELATED_shared-index: Defense depends on git index state that multiple sessions modify concurrently
concurrency_safety_taxonomy.CORRELATED_external: Defense cannot prevent failures outside its scope (e.g., WSL filesystem)
concurrency_safety_taxonomy.CORRELATED_defense-as-friction: Defense generates false positives that incentivize bypass workarounds
